Name: UCharacterMovementComponent
Description:
CharacterMovementComponent handles movement logic for the associated Character owner.
  It supports various movement modes including: walking, falling, swimming, flying, custom.
 
  Movement is affected primarily by current Velocity and Acceleration. Acceleration is updated each frame
  based on the input vector accumulated thus far (see UPawnMovementComponent::GetPendingInputVector()).
 
  Networking is fully implemented, with server-client correction and prediction included.
 
  @see ACharacter, UPawnMovementComponent

Variables.Name: CharacterOwner
Variables.Description: Character movement component belongs to

Variables.Name: bApplyGravityWhileJumping
Variables.Description:
Apply gravity while the character is actively jumping (e.g. holding the jump key).
	 	Helps remove frame-rate dependent jump height, but may alter base jump height.

Variables.Name: GravityScale
Variables.Description: Custom gravity scale. Gravity is multiplied by this amount for the character.

Variables.Name: MaxStepHeight
Variables.Description: Maximum height character can step up

Variables.Name: JumpZVelocity
Variables.Description: Initial velocity (instantaneous vertical acceleration) when jumping.

Variables.Name: JumpOffJumpZFactor
Variables.Description: Fraction of JumpZVelocity to use when automatically "jumping off" of a base actor that's not allowed to be a base for a character. (For example, if you're not allowed to stand on other players.)

Variables.Name: WalkableFloorAngle
Variables.Description: Max angle in degrees of a walkable surface. Any greater than this and it is too steep to be walkable.

Variables.Name: WalkableFloorZ
Variables.Description: Minimum Z value for floor normal. If less, not a walkable surface. Computed from WalkableFloorAngle.

Variables.Name: MovementMode
Variables.Description:
Actor's current movement mode (walking, falling, etc).
	     - walking:  Walking on a surface, under the effects of friction, and able to "step up" barriers. Vertical velocity is zero.
	     - falling:  Falling under the effects of gravity, after jumping or walking off the edge of a surface.
	     - flying:   Flying, ignoring the effects of gravity.
	     - swimming: Swimming through a fluid volume, under the effects of gravity and buoyancy.
	     - custom:   User-defined custom movement mode, including many possible sub-modes.
	  This is automatically replicated through the Character owner and for client-server movement functions.
	  @see SetMovementMode(), CustomMovementMode

Variables.Name: CustomMovementMode
OldBaseLocation
Variables.Description: Saved location of object we are standing on, for UpdateBasedMovement() to determine if base moved in the last frame, and therefore pawn needs an update.

Variables.Name: OldBaseQuat
Variables.Description: Saved location of object we are standing on, for UpdateBasedMovement() to determine if base moved in the last frame, and therefore pawn needs an update.

Variables.Name: GroundFriction
Variables.Description:
Setting that affects movement control. Higher values allow faster changes in direction.
	  If bUseSeparateBrakingFriction is false, also affects the ability to stop more quickly when braking (whenever Acceleration is zero), where it is multiplied by BrakingFrictionFactor.
	  When braking, this property allows you to control how much friction is applied when moving across the ground, applying an opposing force that scales with current velocity.
	  This can be used to simulate slippery surfaces such as ice or oil by changing the value (possibly based on the material pawn is standing on).
	  @see BrakingDecelerationWalking, BrakingFriction, bUseSeparateBrakingFriction, BrakingFrictionFactor

Variables.Name: MaxWalkSpeed
Variables.Description: The maximum ground speed when walking. Also determines maximum lateral speed when falling.

Variables.Name: MaxWalkSpeedCrouched
Variables.Description: The maximum ground speed when walking and crouched.

Variables.Name: MaxSwimSpeed
Variables.Description: The maximum swimming speed.

Variables.Name: MaxFlySpeed
Variables.Description: The maximum flying speed.

Variables.Name: MaxCustomMovementSpeed
Variables.Description: The maximum speed when using Custom movement mode.

Variables.Name: MaxAcceleration
Variables.Description: Max Acceleration (rate of change of velocity)

Variables.Name: MinAnalogWalkSpeed
Variables.Description: The ground speed that we should accelerate up to when walking at minimum analog stick tilt

Variables.Name: BrakingFrictionFactor
BrakingFriction
bUseSeparateBrakingFriction
Variables.Description:
If true, BrakingFriction will be used to slow the character to a stop (when there is no Acceleration).
	  If false, braking uses the same friction passed to CalcVelocity() (ie GroundFriction when walking), multiplied by BrakingFrictionFactor.
	  This setting applies to all movement modes; if only desired in certain modes, consider toggling it when movement modes change.
	  @see BrakingFriction

Variables.Name: BrakingDecelerationWalking
Variables.Description:
Deceleration when walking and not applying acceleration. This is a constant opposing force that directly lowers velocity by a constant value.
	  @see GroundFriction, MaxAcceleration

Variables.Name: BrakingDecelerationFalling
Variables.Description:
Lateral deceleration when falling and not applying acceleration.
	  @see MaxAcceleration

Variables.Name: BrakingDecelerationSwimming
Variables.Description:
Deceleration when swimming and not applying acceleration.
	  @see MaxAcceleration

Variables.Name: BrakingDecelerationFlying
Variables.Description:
Deceleration when flying and not applying acceleration.
	  @see MaxAcceleration

Variables.Name: AirControl
Variables.Description:
When falling, amount of lateral movement control available to the character.
	  0 = no control, 1 = full control at max speed of MaxWalkSpeed.

Variables.Name: AirControlBoostMultiplier
Variables.Description:
When falling, multiplier applied to AirControl when lateral velocity is less than AirControlBoostVelocityThreshold.
	  Setting this to zero will disable air control boosting. Final result is clamped at 1.

Variables.Name: AirControlBoostVelocityThreshold
Variables.Description:
When falling, if lateral velocity magnitude is less than this value, AirControl is multiplied by AirControlBoostMultiplier.
	  Setting this to zero will disable air control boosting.

Variables.Name: FallingLateralFriction
CrouchedHalfHeight
Variables.Description: Collision half-height when crouching (component scale is applied separately)

Variables.Name: Buoyancy
Variables.Description: Water buoyancy. A ratio (1.0 = neutral buoyancy, 0.0 = no buoyancy)

Variables.Name: PerchRadiusThreshold
Variables.Description:
Don't allow the character to perch on the edge of a surface if the contact is this close to the edge of the capsule.
	  Note that characters will not fall off if they are within MaxStepHeight of a walkable surface below.

Variables.Name: PerchAdditionalHeight
Variables.Description:
When perching on a ledge, add this additional distance to MaxStepHeight when determining how high above a walkable floor we can perch.
	  Note that we still enforce MaxStepHeight to start the step up; this just allows the character to hang off the edge or step slightly higher off the floor.
	  (@see PerchRadiusThreshold)

Variables.Name: RotationRate
Variables.Description: Change in rotation per second, used when UseControllerDesiredRotation or OrientRotationToMovement are true. Set a negative value for infinite rotation rate and instant turns.

Variables.Name: bUseControllerDesiredRotation
Variables.Description:
If true, smoothly rotate the Character toward the Controller's desired rotation (typically Controller->ControlRotation), using RotationRate as the rate of rotation change. Overridden by OrientRotationToMovement.
	  Normally you will want to make sure that other settings are cleared, such as bUseControllerRotationYaw on the Character.

Variables.Name: bOrientRotationToMovement
Variables.Description:
If true, rotate the Character toward the direction of acceleration, using RotationRate as the rate of rotation change. Overrides UseControllerDesiredRotation.
	  Normally you will want to make sure that other settings are cleared, such as bUseControllerRotationYaw on the Character.

Variables.Name: bSweepWhileNavWalking
Variables.Description:
Whether or not the character should sweep for collision geometry while walking.
	  @see USceneComponent::MoveComponent.

Variables.Name: bMovementInProgress
bEnableScopedMovementUpdates
bForceMaxAccel
Variables.Description: Ignores size of acceleration component, and forces max acceleration to drive character at full velocity.

Variables.Name: bRunPhysicsWithNoController
Variables.Description:
If true, movement will be performed even if there is no Controller for the Character owner.
	  Normally without a Controller, movement will be aborted and velocity and acceleration are zeroed if the character is walking.
	  Characters that are spawned without a Controller but with this flag enabled will initialize the movement mode to DefaultLandMovementMode or DefaultWaterMovementMode appropriately.
	  @see DefaultLandMovementMode, DefaultWaterMovementMode

Variables.Name: bForceNextFloorCheck
Variables.Description:
Force the Character in MOVE_Walking to do a check for a valid floor even if he hasn't moved. Cleared after next floor check.
	  Normally if bAlwaysCheckFloor is false we try to avoid the floor check unless some conditions are met, but this can be used to force the next check to always run.

Variables.Name: bShrinkProxyCapsule
Variables.Description:
If true, the capsule needs to be shrunk on this simulated proxy, to avoid replication rounding putting us in geometry.
	   Whenever this is set to true, this will cause the capsule to be shrunk again on the next update, and then set to false.

Variables.Name: bCanWalkOffLedges
Variables.Description: If true, Character can walk off a ledge.

Variables.Name: bCanWalkOffLedgesWhenCrouching
Variables.Description: If true, Character can walk off a ledge when crouching.

Variables.Name: bNetworkSmoothingComplete
Variables.Description:
Signals that smoothed positionrotation has reached target, and no more smoothing is necessary until a future update.
	  This is used as an optimization to skip calls to SmoothClientPosition() when true. SmoothCorrection() sets it false when a new network update is received.
	  SmoothClientPosition_Interpolate() sets this to true when the interpolation reaches the target, before one last call to SmoothClientPosition_UpdateVisuals().
	  If this is not desired, override SmoothClientPosition() to always set this to false to avoid this feature.

Variables.Name: bNetworkSkipProxyPredictionOnNetUpdate
Variables.Description:
Whether we skip prediction on frames where a proxy receives a network update. This can avoid expensive prediction on those frames,
	 with the side-effect of predicting with a frame of additional latency.

Variables.Name: bForceNoSimulatePrediction
Variables.Description: Whether we skip prediction on simulate movement, only interpolate from server replicated movement

Variables.Name: bDeferUpdateMoveComponent
Variables.Description: true to update CharacterOwner and UpdatedComponent after movement ends

Variables.Name: DeferredUpdatedMoveComponent
Variables.Description: What to update CharacterOwner and UpdatedComponent after movement ends

Variables.Name: MaxOutOfWaterStepHeight
Variables.Description: Maximum step height for getting out of water

Variables.Name: OutofWaterZ
Variables.Description: Z velocity applied when pawn tries to get out of water

Variables.Name: Mass
Variables.Description: Mass of pawn (for when momentum is imparted to it).

Variables.Name: bEnablePhysicsInteraction
Variables.Description: If enabled, the player will interact with physics objects when walking into them.

Variables.Name: bTouchForceScaledToMass
Variables.Description: If enabled, the TouchForceFactor is applied per kg mass of the affected object.

Variables.Name: bPushForceScaledToMass
Variables.Description: If enabled, the PushForceFactor is applied per kg mass of the affected object.

Variables.Name: bPushForceUsingZOffset
Variables.Description: If enabled, the PushForce location is moved using PushForcePointZOffsetFactor. Otherwise simply use the impact point.

Variables.Name: bScalePushForceToVelocity
Variables.Description:
If enabled, the applied push force will try to get the physics object to the same velocity than the player, not faster. This will only
		scale the force down, it will never apply more force than defined by PushForceFactor.

Variables.Name: StandingDownwardForceScale
Variables.Description: Force applied to objects we stand on (due to Mass and Gravity) is scaled by this amount.

Variables.Name: InitialPushForceFactor
Variables.Description: Initial impulse force to apply when the player bounces into a blocking physics object.

Variables.Name: PushForceFactor
Variables.Description: Force to apply when the player collides with a blocking physics object.

Variables.Name: PushForcePointZOffsetFactor
Variables.Description: Z-Offset for the position the force is applied to. 0.0f is the center of the physics object, 1.0f is the top and -1.0f is the bottom of the object.

Variables.Name: TouchForceFactor
Variables.Description: Force to apply to physics objects that are touched by the player.

Variables.Name: MinTouchForce
Variables.Description: Minimum Force applied to touched physics objects. If < 0.0f, there is no minimum.

Variables.Name: MaxTouchForce
Variables.Description: Maximum force applied to touched physics objects. If < 0.0f, there is no maximum.

Variables.Name: RepulsionForce
Variables.Description: Force per kg applied constantly to all overlapping components.

Variables.Name: CrouchedSpeedMultiplier_DEPRECATED
Variables.Description: Multiplier to max ground speed to use when crouched

Variables.Name: Acceleration
Variables.Description:
Current acceleration vector (with magnitude).
	  This is calculated each update based on the input vector and the constraints of MaxAcceleration and the current movement mode.

Variables.Name: LastUpdateLocation
Variables.Description: Location after last PerformMovement or SimulateMovement update. Used internally to detect changes in position from outside character movement to try to validate the current floor.

Variables.Name: LastUpdateRotation
Variables.Description: Rotation after last PerformMovement or SimulateMovement update.

Variables.Name: LastUpdateVelocity
Variables.Description: Velocity after last PerformMovement or SimulateMovement update. Used internally to detect changes in velocity from external sources.

Variables.Name: ServerLastTransformUpdateTimeStamp
Variables.Description: Timestamp when location or rotation last changed during an update. Only valid on the server.

Variables.Name: PendingImpulseToApply
Variables.Description: Accumulated impulse to be added next tick.

Variables.Name: PendingForceToApply
Variables.Description: Accumulated force to be added next tick.

Variables.Name: AnalogInputModifier
Variables.Description: Modifier to applied to values such as acceleration and max speed due to analog input.

Variables.Name: LastStuckWarningTime
Variables.Description: Used for throttling "stuck in geometry" logging.

Variables.Name: MaxSimulationTimeStep
Variables.Description:
Max time delta for each discrete simulation step.
	  Used primarily in the the more advanced movement modes that break up larger time steps (usually those applying gravity such as falling and walking).
	  Lowering this value can address issues with fast-moving objects or complex collision scenarios, at the cost of performance.
	 
	  WARNING: if (MaxSimulationTimeStep  MaxSimulationIterations) is too low for the min framerate, the last simulation step may exceed MaxSimulationTimeStep to complete the simulation.
	  @see MaxSimulationIterations

Variables.Name: MaxSimulationIterations
Variables.Description:
Max number of iterations used for each discrete simulation step.
	  Used primarily in the the more advanced movement modes that break up larger time steps (usually those applying gravity such as falling and walking).
	  Increasing this value can address issues with fast-moving objects or complex collision scenarios, at the cost of performance.
	 
	  WARNING: if (MaxSimulationTimeStep  MaxSimulationIterations) is too low for the min framerate, the last simulation step may exceed MaxSimulationTimeStep to complete the simulation.
	  @see MaxSimulationTimeStep

Variables.Name: MaxDepenetrationWithGeometry
MaxDepenetrationWithGeometryAsProxy
MaxDepenetrationWithPawn
Variables.Description:
Max distance we are allowed to depenetrate when moving out of other Pawns.
	 @see MaxDepenetrationWithGeometry, MaxDepenetrationWithGeometryAsProxy, MaxDepenetrationWithPawnAsProxy

Variables.Name: MaxDepenetrationWithPawnAsProxy
NetworkSimulatedSmoothLocationTime
Variables.Description: How long to take to smoothly interpolate from the old pawn position on the client to the corrected one sent by the server. Not used by Linear smoothing.

Variables.Name: NetworkSimulatedSmoothRotationTime
Variables.Description: How long to take to smoothly interpolate from the old pawn rotation on the client to the corrected one sent by the server. Not used by Linear smoothing.

Variables.Name: ListenServerNetworkSimulatedSmoothLocationTime
Variables.Description: Similar setting as NetworkSimulatedSmoothLocationTime but only used on Listen servers.

Variables.Name: ListenServerNetworkSimulatedSmoothRotationTime
Variables.Description: Similar setting as NetworkSimulatedSmoothRotationTime but only used on Listen servers.

Variables.Name: NetProxyShrinkRadius
Variables.Description:
Shrink simulated proxy capsule radius by this amount, to account for network rounding that may cause encroachment. Changing during gameplay is not supported.
	  @see AdjustProxyCapsuleSize()

Variables.Name: NetProxyShrinkHalfHeight
Variables.Description:
Shrink simulated proxy capsule half height by this amount, to account for network rounding that may cause encroachment. Changing during gameplay is not supported.
	  @see AdjustProxyCapsuleSize()

Variables.Name: NetworkMaxSmoothUpdateDistance
Variables.Description: Maximum distance character is allowed to lag behind server location when interpolating between updates.

Variables.Name: NetworkNoSmoothUpdateDistance
Variables.Description: Maximum distance beyond which character is teleported to the new server location without any smoothing.

Variables.Name: NetworkSmoothingMode
Variables.Description: Smoothing mode for simulated proxies in network game.

Variables.Name: LedgeCheckThreshold
Variables.Description: Used in determining if pawn is going off ledge.  If the ledge is "shorter" than this value then the pawn will be able to walk off it.

Variables.Name: JumpOutOfWaterPitch
Variables.Description: When exiting water, jump if control pitch angle is this high or above.

Variables.Name: CurrentFloor
Variables.Description: Information about the floor the Character is standing on (updated only during walking movement).

Variables.Name: DefaultLandMovementMode
DefaultWaterMovementMode
GroundMovementMode
bMaintainHorizontalGroundVelocity
Variables.Description:
If true, walking movement always maintains horizontal velocity when moving up ramps, which causes movement up ramps to be faster parallel to the ramp surface.
	  If false, then walking movement maintains velocity magnitude parallel to the ramp surface.

Variables.Name: bImpartBaseVelocityX
Variables.Description: If true, impart the base actor's X velocity when falling off it (which includes jumping)

Variables.Name: bImpartBaseVelocityY
Variables.Description: If true, impart the base actor's Y velocity when falling off it (which includes jumping)

Variables.Name: bImpartBaseVelocityZ
Variables.Description: If true, impart the base actor's Z velocity when falling off it (which includes jumping)

Variables.Name: bImpartBaseAngularVelocity
bJustTeleported
Variables.Description: Used by movement code to determine if a change in position is based on normal movement or a teleport. If not a teleport, velocity can be recomputed based on the change in position.

Variables.Name: bNetworkUpdateReceived
Variables.Description: True when a network replication update is received for simulated proxies.

Variables.Name: bNetworkMovementModeChanged
Variables.Description: True when the networked movement mode has been replicated.

Variables.Name: bIgnoreClientMovementErrorChecksAndCorrection
Variables.Description:
True when we should ignore server location difference checks for client error on this movement component
	  This can be useful when character is moving at extreme speeds for a duration and you need it to look
	  smooth on clients. Make sure to disable when done, as this would break this character's server-client
	  movement correction.

Variables.Name: bNotifyApex
Variables.Description:
If true, event NotifyJumpApex() to CharacterOwner's controller when at apex of jump. Is cleared when event is triggered.
	  By default this is off, and if you want the event to fire you typically set it to true when movement mode changes to "Falling" from another mode (see OnMovementModeChanged).

Variables.Name: bCheatFlying
Variables.Description: Instantly stop when in flying mode and no acceleration is being applied.

Variables.Name: bWantsToCrouch
Variables.Description: If true, try to crouch (or keep crouching) on next update. If false, try to stop crouching on next update.

Variables.Name: bCrouchMaintainsBaseLocation
Variables.Description:
If true, crouching should keep the base of the capsule in place by lowering the center of the shrunken capsule. If false, the base of the capsule moves up and the center stays in place.
	  The same behavior applies when the character uncrouches: if true, the base is kept in the same location and the center moves up. If false, the capsule grows and only moves up if the base impacts something.
	  By default this variable is set when the movement mode changes: set to true when walking and false otherwise. Feel free to override the behavior when the movement mode changes.

Variables.Name: bIgnoreBaseRotation
Variables.Description:
Whether the character ignores changes in rotation of the base it is standing on.
	  If true, the character maintains current world rotation.
	  If false, the character rotates with the moving base.

Variables.Name: bFastAttachedMove
Variables.Description:
Set this to true if riding on a moving base that you know is clear from non-moving world obstructions.
	  Optimization to avoid sweeps during based movement, use with care.

Variables.Name: bAlwaysCheckFloor
Variables.Description:
Whether we always force floor checks for stationary Characters while walking.
	  Normally floor checks are avoided if possible when not moving, but this can be used to force them if there are use-cases where they are being skipped erroneously
	  (such as objects moving up into the character from below).

Variables.Name: bUseFlatBaseForFloorChecks
Variables.Description:
Performs floor checks as if the character is using a shape with a flat base.
	  This avoids the situation where characters slowly lower off the side of a ledge (as their capsule 'balances' on the edge).

Variables.Name: bPerformingJumpOff
Variables.Description: Used to prevent reentry of JumpOff()

Variables.Name: bWantsToLeaveNavWalking
Variables.Description: Used to safely leave NavWalking movement mode

Variables.Name: bUseRVOAvoidance
Variables.Description: If set, component will use RVO avoidance. This only runs on the server.

Variables.Name: bRequestedMoveUseAcceleration
Variables.Description:
Should use acceleration for path following?
	  If true, acceleration is applied when path following to reach the target velocity.
	  If false, path following velocity is set directly, disregarding acceleration.

Variables.Name: bIsNavWalkingOnServer
Variables.Description: Set on clients when server's movement mode is NavWalking

Variables.Name: bHasRequestedVelocity
Variables.Description: Was velocity requested by path following?

Variables.Name: bRequestedMoveWithMaxSpeed
Variables.Description: Was acceleration requested to be always max speed?

Variables.Name: bWasAvoidanceUpdated
Variables.Description: Was avoidance updated in this frame?

Variables.Name: bUseRVOPostProcess
Variables.Description: if set, PostProcessAvoidanceVelocity will be called

Variables.Name: bDeferUpdateBasedMovement
Variables.Description: Flag set in pre-physics update to indicate that based movement should be updated post-physics

Variables.Name: bProjectNavMeshWalking
Variables.Description: Whether to raycast to underlying geometry to better conform navmesh-walking characters

Variables.Name: bProjectNavMeshOnBothWorldChannels
Variables.Description: Use both WorldStatic and WorldDynamic channels for NavWalking geometry conforming

Variables.Name: AvoidanceLockVelocity
Variables.Description: forced avoidance velocity, used when AvoidanceLockTimer is > 0

Variables.Name: AvoidanceLockTimer
Variables.Description: remaining time of avoidance velocity lock

Variables.Name: RequestedVelocity
Variables.Description:
Velocity requested by path following.
	  @see RequestDirectMove()

Variables.Name: AvoidanceUID
Variables.Description: No default value, for now it's assumed to be valid if GetAvoidanceManager() returns non-NULL.

Variables.Name: AvoidanceGroup
Variables.Description: Moving actor's group mask

Variables.Name: GroupsToAvoid
Variables.Description: Will avoid other agents if they are in one of specified groups

Variables.Name: GroupsToIgnore
Variables.Description: Will NOT avoid other agents if they are in one of specified groups, higher priority than GroupsToAvoid

Variables.Name: AvoidanceWeight
Variables.Description: De facto default value 0.5 (due to that being the default in the avoidance registration function), indicates RVO behavior.

Variables.Name: PendingLaunchVelocity
Variables.Description: Temporarily holds launch velocity when pawn is to be launched so it happens at end of movement.

Variables.Name: CachedProjectedNavMeshHitResult
Variables.Description: Last valid projected hit result from raycast to geometry from navmesh

Variables.Name: NavMeshProjectionInterval
Variables.Description: How often we should raycast to project from navmesh to underlying geometry

Variables.Name: NavMeshProjectionInterpSpeed
Variables.Description: Speed at which to interpolate agent navmesh offset between traces. 0: Instant (no interp) > 0: Interp speed")

Variables.Name: NavMeshProjectionHeightScaleUp
Variables.Description:
Scale of the total capsule height to use for projection from navmesh to underlying geometry in the upward direction.
	  In other words, start the trace at [CapsuleHeight  NavMeshProjectionHeightScaleUp] above nav mesh.

Variables.Name: NavMeshProjectionHeightScaleDown
Variables.Description:
Scale of the total capsule height to use for projection from navmesh to underlying geometry in the downward direction.
	  In other words, trace down to [CapsuleHeight  NavMeshProjectionHeightScaleDown] below nav mesh.

Variables.Name: NavWalkingFloorDistTolerance
Variables.Description: Ignore small differences in ground height between server and client data during NavWalking mode

Variables.Name: PostPhysicsTickFunction
Variables.Description: Post-physics tick function for this character

Variables.Name: MinTimeBetweenTimeStampResets
Variables.Description:
Minimum time between client TimeStamp resets.
	 So we trigger a TimeStamp reset at regular intervals to maintain a high level of accuracy.

Variables.Name: CurrentRootMotion
Variables.Description: Root Motion Group containing active root motion sources being applied to movement

Variables.Name: RootMotionParams
Variables.Description:
Animation root motion (special case for now)
	
	 Root Motion movement params. Holds result of anim montage root motion during PerformMovement(), and is overridden
	   during autonomous move playback to force historical root motion for MoveAutonomous() calls

Variables.Name: AnimRootMotionVelocity
Variables.Description: Velocity extracted from RootMotionParams when there is anim root motion active. Invalid to use when HasAnimRootMotion() returns false.

Variables.Name: bWasSimulatingRootMotion
Variables.Description: True when SimulatedProxies are simulating RootMotion

Functions.Name: SetAvoidanceEnabled
Functions.Description: Change avoidance state and registers in RVO manager if needed

Functions.Name: GetCharacterOwner
Functions.Description: Get the Character that owns UpdatedComponent.

Functions.Name: SetMovementMode
Functions.Description: Change movement mode.

Params.Name: NewCustomMode
Params.Description: The new custom sub-mode, only applicable if NewMovementMode is Custom.

Functions.Name: SetGroundMovementMode
Functions.Description:
Set movement mode to use when returning to walking movement (either MOVE_Walking or MOVE_NavWalking).
	  If movement mode is currently one of Walking or NavWalking, this will also change the current movement mode (via SetMovementMode())
	  if the new mode is not the current ground mode.

Params.Name: NewGroundMovementMode
Params.Description: New ground movement mode. Must be either MOVE_Walking or MOVE_NavWalking, other values are ignored.

Functions.Name: GetGroundMovementMode
Functions.Description: Get current GroundMovementMode value.

Return.Description: true if the character is in the 'Walking' movement mode.

Functions.Name: DisableMovement
Functions.Description: Make movement impossible (sets movement mode to MOVE_None).

Functions.Name: HasValidData
Functions.Description: Return true if we have a valid CharacterOwner and UpdatedComponent.

Functions.Name: GetMovementBase
Functions.Description: Return PrimitiveComponent we are based on (standing and walking on).

Functions.Name: MaybeUpdateBasedMovement
Functions.Description: Update or defer updating of position based on Base movement

Functions.Name: MaybeSaveBaseLocation
Functions.Description: Call SaveBaseLocation() if not deferring updates (bDeferUpdateBasedMovement is false).

Functions.Name: GetImpartedMovementBaseVelocity
Functions.Description:
If we have a movement base, get the velocity that should be imparted by that base, usually when jumping off of it.
	  Only applies the components of the velocity enabled by bImpartBaseVelocityX, bImpartBaseVelocityY, bImpartBaseVelocityZ.

Functions.Name: CalcVelocity
Functions.Description:
Updates Velocity and Acceleration based on the current state, applying the effects of friction and acceleration or deceleration. Does not apply gravity.
	  This is used internally during movement updates. Normally you don't need to call this from outside code, but you might want to use it for custom movement modes.

Params.Name: Friction
Params.Description: coefficient of friction when not accelerating, or in the direction opposite acceleration.

Params.Name: bFluid
Params.Description: true if moving through a fluid, causing Friction to always be applied regardless of acceleration.

Params.Name: BrakingDeceleration
Params.Description: deceleration applied when not accelerating, or when exceeding max velocity.

Functions.Name: GetMaxJumpHeight
Functions.Description:
Compute the max jump height based on the JumpZVelocity velocity and gravity.
	 	This does not take into account the CharacterOwner's MaxJumpHoldTime.

Functions.Name: GetMaxJumpHeightWithJumpTime
Functions.Description:
Compute the max jump height based on the JumpZVelocity velocity and gravity.
	 	This does take into account the CharacterOwner's MaxJumpHoldTime.

Return.Description: Maximum acceleration for the current state.
Maximum acceleration for the current state, based on MaxAcceleration and any additional modifiers.
Maximum acceleration for the current state.
Maximum deceleration for the current state when braking (ie when there is no acceleration).
Current acceleration, computed from input vector each update.
Modifier [0..1] based on the magnitude of the last input vector, which is used to modify the acceleration and max speed during movement.
true if we can step up on the actor in the given FHitResult.

Functions.Name: SetBase
Functions.Description: Update the base of the character, which is the PrimitiveComponent we are standing on.

Functions.Name: SetBaseFromFloor
Functions.Description: Update the base of the character, using the given floor result if it is walkable, or null if not. Calls SetBase().

Functions.Name: ClearAccumulatedForces
Functions.Description: Clears forces accumulated through AddImpulse() and AddForce(), and also pending launch velocity.

Functions.Name: HasAccumulatedForcesOrLaunch
Functions.Description:
Add by zoranouyang
	 Is there AddImpulse() or AddForce() or Launch()?

Functions.Name: AddImpulse
Functions.Description:
Add impulse to character. Impulses are accumulated each tick and applied together
	  so multiple calls to this function will accumulate.
	  An impulse is an instantaneous force, usually applied once. If you want to continually apply
	  forces each frame, use AddForce().
	  Note that changing the momentum of characters like this can change the movement mode.

Params.Name: bVelocityChange
Params.Description: Whether or not the impulse is relative to mass.

Functions.Name: AddForce
Functions.Description:
Add force to character. Forces are accumulated each tick and applied together
	  so multiple calls to this function will accumulate.
	  Forces are scaled depending on timestep, so they can be applied each frame. If you want an
	  instantaneous force, use AddImpulse.
	  Adding a force always takes the actor's mass into account.
	  Note that changing the momentum of characters like this can change the movement mode.

Return.Description: The distance from the edge of the capsule within which we don't allow the character to perch on the edge of a surface.

Functions.Name: GetValidPerchRadius
Functions.Description:
Returns the radius within which we can stand on the edge of a surface without falling (if this is a walkable surface).
	  Simply computed as the capsule radius minus the result of GetPerchRadiusThreshold().

Functions.Name: IsWalkable
Functions.Description: Return true if the hit result should be considered a walkable surface for the character.

Functions.Name: K2_GetWalkableFloorAngle
Functions.Description: Get the max angle in degrees of a walkable surface for the character.

Functions.Name: SetWalkableFloorAngle
Functions.Description: Set the max angle in degrees of a walkable surface for the character. Also computes WalkableFloorZ.

Functions.Name: K2_GetWalkableFloorZ
Functions.Description: Get the Z component of the normal of the steepest walkable surface for the character. Any lower than this and it is not walkable.

Functions.Name: SetWalkableFloorZ
Functions.Description: Set the Z component of the normal of the steepest walkable surface for the character. Also computes WalkableFloorAngle.

Functions.Name: K2_FindFloor
Functions.Description:
Sweeps a vertical trace to find the floor for the capsule at the given location. Will attempt to perch if ShouldComputePerchResult() returns true for the downward sweep result.
	 No floor will be found if collision is disabled on the capsule!

Params.Name: CapsuleLocation
Params.Description: Location where the capsule sweep should originate

Functions.Name: K2_ComputeFloorDist
Functions.Description:
Compute distance to the floor from bottom sphere of capsule and store the result in FloorResult.
	 This distance is the swept distance of the capsule to the first point impacted by the lower hemisphere, or distance from the bottom of the capsule in the case of a line trace.
	 This function does not care if collision is disabled on the capsule (unlike FindFloor).

Params.Name: CapsuleLocation
Params.Description: Location where the capsule sweep should originate

Params.Name: LineDistance
Params.Description: If non-zero, max distance to test for a simple line check from the capsule base. Used only if the sweep test fails to find a walkable floor, and only returns a valid result if the impact normal is a walkable normal.

Params.Name: SweepDistance
Params.Description: If non-zero, max distance to use when sweeping a capsule downwards for the test. MUST be greater than or equal to the line distance.

Params.Name: SweepRadius
Params.Description: The radius to use for sweep tests. Should be <= capsule radius.

Functions.Name: CapsuleTouched
Functions.Description: Called when the collision capsule touches another primitive component

Functions.Name: SetSmoothNetUpdateRotationTimeTemporaty
Functions.Description:
Add by zoranouyang
	 临时的SmoothNetUpdateRotationTime，用于部分情况下需要一段时间内修改一下模拟端Rotation插值速度
	 主要还是以NetworkSimulatedSmoothRotationTime配置为主
	 注意：本值要记得还原到-1，表示不生效

Params.Name: InSmoothNetUpdateRotationTime
Params.Description: 模拟端的Rotation插值时间，默认值-1表示使用NetworkSimulatedSmoothRotationTime配置的值

Functions.Name: ServerMove
Functions.Description: Replicated function sent by client to server - contains client movement and view info.

Functions.Name: ServerMoveDual
Functions.Description: Replicated function sent by client to server - contains client movement and view info for two moves.

Functions.Name: ServerMoveDualHybridRootMotion
Functions.Description: Replicated function sent by client to server - contains client movement and view info for two moves. First move is non root motion, second is root motion.

Functions.Name: ServerMoveOld
Functions.Description: Resending an (important) old move. Process it if not already processed.

Functions.Name: ClientAckGoodMove
Functions.Description: If no client adjustment is needed after processing received ServerMove(), ack the good move so client can remove it from SavedMoves

Functions.Name: ClientAdjustPosition
Functions.Description: Replicate position correction to client, associated with a timestamped servermove.  Client will replay subsequent moves after applying adjustment.

Functions.Name: ClientVeryShortAdjustPosition
Functions.Description: Bandwidth saving version, when velocity is zeroed

Functions.Name: ClientAdjustRootMotionPosition
Functions.Description: Replicate position correction to client when using root motion for movement. (animation root motion specific)

Functions.Name: ClientAdjustRootMotionSourcePosition
Functions.Description: Replicate root motion source correction to client when using root motion for movement.
